data.title: 'Momenum Stays Ahead of Valuation,' Says Analyst on Rocket Lab Stock (RKLB)
data.description:
Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) stock has been on a tear, posting one of the strongest performances among its space-sector peers over the past year. The company—which builds rockets, satellites, and space infrastructure for defense—has rallied on the back of defense contract wins, its booming Space Systems business, and an increasingly bullish outlook for Neutron, its medium-lift launch vehicle.

data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: 'The Genie Is Out of the Bottle': Buffett Compares AI to Nuclear Weapons
data.description:
Legendary investor Warren Buffett recently warned that the fast growth of artificial intelligence is similar to the early days of nuclear weapons. Speaking during a two-hour CNBC special, the former CEO of conglomerate Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) said that even the smartest people working on AI admit they do not know where the technology is going. He explained that, unlike an explorer who can turn back, AI cannot be put away once it is released.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

To illustrate this risk, Buffett pointed to Albert Einstein's famous quote that the atomic bomb changed everything in the world except how people think. Building on that idea, Buffett compared AI's uncertainty to how nuclear weapons spread over time. He said what was once expected to stay in the hands of one country has now grown to eight or nine nations, some of which are very unstable. That, he warned, makes the danger even greater, because powerful technology in the wrong hands can be extremely destructive.

At the same time, Buffett said that he would spend his entire fortune to reduce the nuclear threat if he could, even though he understands why the bomb was considered necessary during World War II. Interestingly, Buffett also explained that worries about nuclear weapons have shaped how he thinks about charity and global safety for many years. In fact, in Berkshire's 2015 annual report, he warned that nuclear, cyber, and biological attacks are the biggest threats to the company and the country. More recently, he has said that AI has huge potential for both good and harm.

Is Berkshire Hathaway Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Berkshire Hathaway stock based on one Buy and one Hold assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average Berkshire Hathaway price target of $538 per share implies 8.9% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Stock Market Today: SPY, QQQ Sell Off on Surprising Inflation Print as Retail Sales Shine
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 ETF (SPY) and the Nasdaq 100 ETF (QQQ) closed lower on Wednesday as investors digested new inflation and retail sales data.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In November, the Producer Price Index (PPI) climbed 0.2% month-over-month, rising from 0.1% in October and in line with the analyst estimate of 0.2%. However, PPI rose 3% year-over-year, topping the estimate of 2.7%. The PPI tracks changes in prices received by producers for goods and services and is often viewed as a leading indicator of consumer inflation. Core PPI, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, was unchanged from the prior month and advanced 3% year-over-year. Analysts had forecast growth of 0.2% and 2.7%, respectively.

A strong consumer spending update wasn't enough to lift the market. In November, retail sales increased 0.6% month-over-month, above the estimate of 0.5% and marking the highest rate of growth since July. At the same time, the U.S. Department of Commerce revised October's retail sales growth to a decline of 0.1% from 0%.

November is generally a strong month for sales, as it marks the beginning of the holiday shopping season and includes Black Friday. From November 1 to December 31, online consumer spending surged 6.8% from the prior year to a record high of $257.8 billion, according to Adobe (ADBE).

Meanwhile, the Supreme Court held its second opinion day of the year, although investors expecting a decision on the landmark Trump tariff case were left empty-handed. The next earliest date that SCOTUS could announce a ruling for the case is Tuesday, although uncertainty remains as the court doesn't preannounce the rulings it will address.

Still, President Trump's tariffs haven't resulted in higher inflation, according to Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia President Anna Paulson. "We've seen progress on underlying inflation that is likely to continue and there is no evidence to date that tariff-induced price increases are leading to broader inflation," she said in prepared remarks for an economic event in Philadelphia on Wednesday. Paulson added that her base case for 2026 involves inflation cooling down to the Fed's target of 2%, a stabilizing labor market, and economic growth of 2%.

The S&P 500 (SPX) closed with a 0.53% loss, while the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) fell by 1.07%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Reports Claim Anthropic and OpenAI Are Preparing to Go Public
data.description:
Multiple media reports claim that artificial intelligence (AI) start-up companies Anthropic and OpenAI are taking steps to hold initial public offerings (IPOs) this year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Times (NYT) was the first to report that Anthropic and OpenAI have taken the early steps toward going public, joining Elon Musk's SpaceX in what could be a blockbuster year for technology IPOs on Wall Street.

According to the reports, Anthropic and OpenAI have each taken the preliminary steps toward holding an IPO in coming months. SpaceX, a satellite and commercial space company, is also moving towards an IPO this year in what could be the biggest market debut ever for a U.S. concern.

Massive Valuations for Anthropic and OpenAI

To date, the most valuable company to ever go public was Saudi Aramco in 2019. That IPO saw the energy giant valued at $1.7 trillion. Anthropic is currently in funding talks that could value it at $350 billion, while OpenAI carries a valuation of $500 billion. SpaceX was most recently valued at $800 billion.

Retail investors are salivating at the prospect of these three companies going public. Analysts are saying that 2026 could usher in an "IPO super cycle" and are referring to the potential IPOs of Anthropic, OpenAI, and Space X as creating "monster listing mania." Anthropic and OpenAI each have deep-pocketed investors.

E-commerce giant Amazon (AMZN) has invested a total of $8 billion in Anthropic, while Microsoft (MSFT) has invested $13 billion into OpenAI.

Is MSFT Stock a Buy?

Microsoft's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 34 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 32 Buy and two Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average price target on MSFT stock of $631.36 implies 36.38% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: How Agentic Commerce Can Fuel Shopify's (SHOP) Market Renaissance
data.description:
Shopify (SHOP) surged last year, so jumping in today feels like you're too late to the party. The stock ripped higher on a wave of AI optimism, and the multiple now sits in a valuation stretch most investors usually avoid.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

Yet when you dig into what the company is actually shipping and where AI-driven commerce is heading, you see it quietly laying the groundwork for high-quality infrastructure. This is not the Shopify of 2019, arguing about themes and plug-ins. It is more like an operating system for the next era of online retail. For this reason, I am bullish on the stock despite its seemingly elevated share price and expensive valuation.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: QBTS, RGTI, IONQ: Top Analyst's Quantum Computing Stock Picks for 2026
data.description:
Mizuho Securities top analyst Vijay Rakesh highlighted key advances in quantum computing for 2026. He reiterated Buy ratings on three pure-play quantum firms: D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Rigetti Computing (RGTI), and IonQ Inc. (IONQ).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

He pointed out some major updates such as RGTI's Cepheus-1-108Q – its first 100+ qubit system arriving in Q1 2026, QBTS' acquisition of Quantum Circuits, and IONQ's strategic expansion through KISTI in South Korea and QuantumBasel collaboration in Switzerland.

Rakesh is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #11 out of 12,040 analysts tracked. He has a 68% success rate and an impressive average return per rating of 44.40%.

QBTS Stock: Progress in Gate-Based Quantum Annealing

Rakesh noted that QBTS' $550 million acquisition of Quantum Circuits, a company specializing in superconducting quantum computers, could speed up its gate-based systems by about 3 years. This deal makes QBTS the first big quantum company with both annealing and gate-model superconducting quantum tech.

He also thinks that QBTS may qualify for more NQI funding (a U.S. government program), with this gate-based system in development. D-Wave recently demoed the first on-chip cryogenic control of gate-model qubits. This breakthrough cuts down cooling parts without losing accuracy and helping to scale up qubits faster.

Rakesh has a $46 price target on QBTS, implying 59.6% upside potential from current levels.

RGTI Stock: Long-Term Roadmap Intact with 108Q System

Rakesh pointed out that RGTI updated the timeline for its 108-qubit system, Cepheus-1-108Q. It is now slated for general availability at the end of Q1 2026 (previously end of 2025). The delay was due to challenges with the "tunable couplers," and extra time needed to test and hit 99% two-qubit gate fidelity.

RGTI also shared improved goals of 99.7% fidelity for the 9-qubit system and 99.6% for the 36-qubit system. Despite the short-term delay, Rakesh believes Rigetti's long-term plan remains on track: a 1,000+ qubit system with 99.8% two-qubit fidelity by end of 2027.

Rakesh maintained his $50 price target on RGTI, implying a massive 104.3% upside potential from current levels.

IONQ Stock: Lucrative Partnerships Cement Long-Term Success

Rakesh noted that IONQ solidified its 2026 pipeline via a 100-qubit Tempo delivery to KISTI (South Korea) and QuantumBasel expansion (Switzerland), lifting the latter's value to over $60 million through 2029. These deals underscore IONQ's trapped-ion leadership in scalability and reliability, with Tempo's algorithmic qubits enabling practical advantage sooner than rivals.

Quantitatively, the roughly 2x value increase in QuantumBasel reflects rising demand, while strategically, international footprints reduce U.S.-centric risks and validate revenue scalability. IonQ also plans to launch a 256-qubit system by 2026 and expand into quantum networking.

Rakesh kept his $90 price target on IONQ, which suggests about 84% upside potential from current levels.

Concluding Thoughts

Rakesh believes that quantum computing is poised for major breakthroughs in 2026 apart from those mentioned above. Catalysts like 30%-40% market growth, $2.7 billion in NQI funding through 2029, cloud service expansions by Amazon Web Services (AMZN)/ Microsoft (MSFT) Azure/Alphabet (GOOGL), IBM's (IBM) predicted quantum advantage, Google's Willow, and Quantinuum's Helios/Apollo by 2029 are all set to bolster the sector this year.

Based on TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool, QBTS and IONQ have a Strong Buy consensus rating, with IONQ offering a higher upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Sinks on Elevated Safe-Haven Demand
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is down by 3.5 bps on Wednesday to 4.136% amid rising safe-haven demand spurred by elevated geopolitical tensions. The yield on Treasuries moves in the opposite direction of their prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Growing unrest in Iran has captured the attention of President Trump, who said earlier this week that "help is on its way" for protesters. That could mark the beginning of American involvement in another Middle Eastern conflict, although Trump hasn't specified how he will address the situation yet. Furthermore, Trump has continued to express interest in acquiring Greenland, drawing pushback from several European leaders.

Stock Market Volatility Leads to Demand for Treasuries

Furthermore, both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) have pulled back in recent days, depressing investors' appetite for risk and shifting capital into safer assets, like bonds and commodities.

An inflation report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) this morning contributed to the market decline, showing Producer Price Index (PPI) rising by 3% annually, above the consensus estimate of 2.7%. While elevated inflation typically pushes yields higher because investors demand greater compensation for lost purchasing power, safe-haven demand dominated on Wednesday, sending yields lower.

data.author: Eddie Pan
